desc: Vaitape is the practical anchor for Bora Bora, giving you the main village base for ferry transfers, shops, restaurants, and lagoon-tour departures while keeping the destination identity clearly tied to Bora Bora rather than a vague island-wide label.
hls: Best simple anchor for Bora Bora logistics because most transfers and day tours orient around Vaitape.
Lagoon colour and motu scenery are strong year-round, but the drier season is usually easier for boat-heavy itineraries.
Bora Bora is transfer-dependent even after arrival, so resort planning and boat timing matter as much as flight timing.
Water-based activities dominate the trip structure more than village sightseeing.
Accommodation is premium-priced most of the year, with especially sharp pressure in the dry season and festive peaks.
Works best for honeymoon, splurge, or short luxury add-on travel rather than budget island hopping.

todo.name: Lagoon snorkel and motu day trip
todo.desc: Use one full day for the classic Bora Bora lagoon circuit rather than fragmenting the trip into short separate boat slots.

todo.name: Matira Beach afternoon
todo.desc: Keep one unstructured beach block for the best light and calmer water rather than overbooking every day.

todo.name: Vaitape harbour and supply stop
todo.desc: Use Vaitape for practical errands, casual waterfront time, and a clearer feel for the island beyond resort transfers.

todo.name: Sunset cruise or dinner transfer
todo.desc: Plan one evening around the lagoon because Bora Bora's strongest atmosphere is often on the water rather than in town.

todo.name: 4x4 interior island tour
todo.desc: Add one land-based contrast day if you want viewpoints and WWII relics beyond reef and beach time.

todo.name: Resort-motu transfer day buffer
todo.desc: Leave slack around arrival and departure because boat transfers, weather, and inter-island timing can eat more time than expected.

wifi.note: Higher-end resorts are usually fine for basic work, but speeds can be inconsistent and boat-transfer logistics make Bora Bora a poor place to rely on always-on connectivity.

prac.fltNote: There is no practical nonstop UK-Bora Bora routing. Typical trips go UK -> Tahiti (PPT) via a long-haul hub, then continue by domestic flight to Bora Bora Airport (BOB), followed by a boat transfer to Vaitape or a resort motu. Even after reaching BOB, this remains a transfer destination rather than a direct-arrival one.
prac.currency: CFP franc (XPF); cards are common in resorts and established operators, but carrying some cash is sensible for smaller town purchases and tips.
prac.lang: French and Tahitian; English is common in resort and activity settings but less universal for every small-town interaction.
prac.visa: French Polynesia follows French overseas-territory entry rules, so check the territory-specific requirements that apply to your passport rather than assuming generic Schengen rules.
alerts: Inter-island timing and boat transfers add more friction than the map suggests, especially on arrival and departure days.
Weather may not ruin the trip, but cloud and wind can materially change lagoon colour and visibility for premium-priced excursions.
Dining and groceries are limited compared with a normal town base, so casual-budget flexibility is lower than on larger islands.

sweet: June to September is usually the easiest window for drier weather and lagoon outings, while May and October can still work well if you want a slightly softer price-and-crowd profile.
